channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: North Korea sent 3,000 more troops to Russia, according to South's assessment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea sent around 3,000 additional troops to Russia in January and February in continued support for Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine, South Korea’s military said Thursday in its latest assessment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Army wife pleads guilty to fatally stabbing infant son in the neck at Georgia military base
item.description: A military wife has pleaded guilty to second-degree murder in the 2023 murder of her infant son at Fort Eisenhower in Georgia, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: SOMERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firefighters make progress on containing Carolinas wildfires but warn danger persists
item.description: Firefighters announced progress Wednesday night on containing two of the largest wildfires burning in the North Carolina mountains, but they warned that fire danger remained from dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Veterans organization gives back to severely wounded 'appreciative' Ukrainian soldiers
item.description: Ret. Air Force Lt. Col. Jim Lorraine spoke to Fox News Digital about his nonprofit's efforts to aid wounded Ukrainian soldiers in the ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Pasadena reaches agreement to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl
item.description: The City of Pasadena has reached an agreement with the organizers of the 2028 Olympic and Paralympic Games to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl Stadium. Men's and women's semifinal and final matches will be held at the historic venue when Los Angeles hosts the games in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge says extreme heat in Texas prisons is unconstitutional but doesn't order they install AC
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional,” but declined to order the state to immediately start installing air conditioning, which could cost billions.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers, according to a U.S. official and federal aviation records.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron said Wednesday that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge says extreme heat in Texas prisons is unconstitutional but doesn't order they install AC
item.description: A federal judge has found the extreme heat in Texas prisons that don’t have air conditioning is “plainly unconstitutional.”.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ICE arrestees claim they were targeted for protest involvement
item.description: Students who acknowledge they participated in pro-Palestinian protests are being targeted by ICE, a complaint says
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: The White House security breach raises concerns among some military spouses and veterans
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — When Alyssa Myatt’s husband served on an aircraft carrier last year, she and other U.S. Navy spouses had to follow strict security protocols that meant driving to the ship’s home port just to learn that its deployment was being extended.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and ALLEN G. BREED, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: The Social Security Administration said Wednesday that people applying for Social Security Disability Insurance, Medicare, or Supplemental Security Income who are not able to use agency’s online portal, can complete their claim entirely over the phone instead of at an office.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Teens arrested after tractor-trailers struck by objects dropped from overpass
item.description: Two juvenile males, aged 16 and 17, have been charged with reckless endangerment after they reportedly threw multiple objects from an I-640 overpass.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retired immigration judge recalls unaccompanied infants in need of lawyers
item.description: A retired federal immigration judge on Tuesday spoke out against the Trump administration for canceling federal funds for lawyers to represent unaccompanied minors who illegally cross the border into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Boston skating community hosts the world championships even as it mourns plane crash victims
item.description: NORWOOD, Mass. (AP) — The chairs where Jinna Han and Spencer Lane used to sit are still there, and covered with gifts: cards and cookies, plants and pictures — anything that might have sparked a connection for their friends and fellow skaters.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family members of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a group trip to Nashville, Tennessee, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KRON4

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Delta Air Lines unveils 2025 trading cards to celebrate 100 years
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A once-every-four-years report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous reports, largely due to investments made during former President Joe Biden's administration.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Former Utah Rep. Mia Love, the first Black Republican woman elected to the US House, has died
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Former U.S. Rep. Mia Love of Utah, a daughter of Haitian immigrants who became the first Black Republican woman elected to Congress, died Sunday. She was 49. Love's family posted news of her death on Love's X account.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Mass layoffs in the federal government raises concerns of potential espionage
item.description: As President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk work to overhaul the federal government, they’re forcing out thousands of workers with insider knowledge and connections who now need a job.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: The Northville Central School District announced the firing of highs school girls basketball head coach Jim Zullo following a postgame incident with one of the Northville players. The incident occurred following Northville's loss in the Class D state championship game.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, ABC 4

item.title: Education secretary says Columbia University's changes put it on track to recover funding
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. Education Secretary Linda McMahon said Columbia University is “on the right track” toward recovering federal funding after the elite New York City university agreed to implement a host of policy changes demanded by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kitty Dukakis, wife of former governor and presidential candidate, dies at 88
item.description: BROOKLINE, Mass. (AP) — Kitty Dukakis, the wife of former Massachusetts governor and Democratic presidential candidate Michael Dukakis, who spoke openly about her struggles with depression and addiction, has died. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father of the last living American hostage in Gaza hopes Trump can bring his son home
item.description: TEL AVIV (AP) — Unlike many families who blame Israel's government for not getting their loved ones released from captivity in Gaza, Adi Alexander is hesitant to point fingers. Pragmatic and measured, the father of the last living American being held hostage by Hamas just wants his son to come home.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman killed during conjugal visit with convicted California murderer, officials say
item.description: A notorious killer serving life in prison without the possibility of parole for the murder of four men in Los Angeles County reportedly strangled his wife to death during a conjugal visit late last year, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9
